pi_items.source: Code analysis of setup_task.sh: Files generated via python-docx (lines 38-56) and converted to ODT (lines 68-70)
Code analysis of task.json line 5 and export_result.sh line 11: OUTPUT_FILE='$OUTPUT_DIR/hydra_manual_master.odm'

Web search and ODF specification: Verified via OpenDocument Technical Specification and OASIS standards. This is the correct MIME type for .odm (Master Document) files per https://www.iana.org/assignments/media-types/application/vnd.oasis.opendocument.text and verified by test ODM file creation.

privileged_info_summary: The task involves assembling a LibreOffice Master Document (.odm) file from three synthetic source documents. The source documents are programmatically generated by setup_task.sh using the python-docx library with content about a fictional 'Hydra-X Hydraulic Power Unit' system (01_safety.odt containing safety warnings about hydraulic fluid, 02_operation.odt containing pump operation procedures with 2000 PSI target pressure, and 03_maintenance.odt containing maintenance schedules with part number H-992). All metadata claims are verified: the working directory is /home/ga/Documents/Hydra_Manual, the expected output filename is hydra_manual_master.odm, and the required MIME type application/vnd.oasis.opendocument.text-master is the correct standard type for ODF Master Documents. The data is entirely synthetic, generated within the setup script, and does not originate from any external public source.
